coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] attestation evidence for spike acceptance criteria will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: attestation evidence for spike acceptance criteria

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC #1) to explicitly require recording any observed behavioral nuances (e.g., context-load semantics, token implications) that affect downstream stories, so the key nuance about context non-reduction is a verifiable spike output.
evidence: 'KEY NUANCE: imports load at launch and do NOT reduce context, so AGENTS.md must itself be lean; the token win comes from de-dup into docs (drives S2).'
Acceptance Criteria contain only three items: PASS/FAIL of import, fallback decision if FAIL, and clean working tree — none covers the context/token-budget nuance or the downstream S2 implication.
ABSENCE: no AC reads 'Record any nuances about context loading behavior that affect downstream stories.'
findings.finding: The de-risking results section surfaces an additional spike output — the architectural nuance that '@AGENTS.md imports load at launch and do NOT reduce context, so AGENTS.md must itself be lean; the token win comes from de-dup into docs (drives S2)' — but no AC covers or verifies this finding. It is a described deliverable with no covering criterion.
findings.location: De-risking results section — 'KEY NUANCE' paragraph; Acceptance Criteria section
scenarios: A downstream story author reading only the ACs might miss the token-budget constraint and produce an AGENTS.md that is too large, defeating the stated benefit.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC #1: 'Confirmed whether @AGENTS.md in CLAUDE.md loads AGENTS.md into context … result recorded as a comment on this ticket with the exact version tested **and any behavioral nuances (e.g., context-load semantics, import depth limits, token-budget implications) relevant to downstream implementation.**'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify and, if possible, empirically validate the 'imports do NOT reduce context' claim: does it mean imports count against the context window identically to inline content (expected), or that some compression/summarization occurs? Record the precise meaning and its implication for AGENTS.md size budgeting before S2 is scoped.
evidence: 'imports "load at launch" and do NOT reduce context' — stated as a documentation-sourced finding, not an empirically measured outcome of the spike
The canary test described (model returned canary verbatim) validates content delivery, but does not measure context window size before/after the import
The earlier note that 'the live doc page had a fetch bug' creates minor residual uncertainty about the doc source for this secondary claim
This claim directly gates a downstream story (S2) by mandating AGENTS.md leanness
findings.finding: The spike's key downstream design driver — 'imports load at launch and do NOT reduce context, so AGENTS.md must itself be lean' — is a capability assertion about Claude Code's context-window behavior that is stated as a documentation finding but was not empirically measured in the described canary test. The canary test confirmed content delivery, not context-budget consumption, yet this claim drives a concrete architectural constraint (leanness of AGENTS.md) propagated to downstream stories.
findings.location: De-risking results — KEY NUANCE paragraph: 'imports "load at launch" and do NOT reduce context, so AGENTS.md must itself be lean; the token win comes from de-dup into docs (drives S2).'
scenarios: If 'no context reduction' is misread (e.g., imports DO consume context budget identically to inline content, which is the expected behavior), downstream stories may under-specify a context-budget budget or size limit for AGENTS.md
If 'token win comes from de-dup into docs' is not validated, S2 could be built on a false premise about context savings
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence clarification to the spike finding: e.g., 'Imports expand inline into the context window (no summarization), so AGENTS.md token count directly adds to session context; the migration's context saving comes solely from removing duplicate content across files, not from any import-time compression.' This removes ambiguity for S2 authors.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-line note to the spike results stating the auth/credential precondition satisfied during the test (e.g., `claude` was authenticated via `<mechanism>`) so the result is reproducible.
evidence: 'the model returned the canary verbatim and with no approval prompt' — live model call implied, auth not mentioned
Acceptance Criteria only requires git status clean; no mention of auth/env setup needed to reproduce
Absence: no statement of CLAUDE_API_KEY, subscription tier, or login session precondition
findings.finding: Auth/HTTPS preconditions for headless Claude Code execution are not stated. The spike reports 'the model returned the canary verbatim,' which implies a live model invocation succeeded — requiring a valid session, API key, or subscription credential — but neither the required credential type nor how it was provisioned is documented.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'Ran headless Claude Code v2.1.211 in an isolated temp dir'
scenarios: A teammate or CI runner tries to reproduce or extend the spike and cannot because they lack undocumented credentials or are in a network-restricted environment
A downstream story inherits this spike as proof-of-mechanism but cannot replicate the test environment
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the 'De-risking results' comment: 'Precondition: `claude` authenticated via [e.g., `claude auth login` / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var / Pro subscription] in the test shell.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the docs URL resolves and the quoted text is accurate (screenshot or archived link), OR explicitly mark the secondary doc-derived claims (4-hop depth limit, approval-prompt rules) as 'doc-asserted, not empirically tested' so downstream stories know which claims need their own verification.
evidence: URL 'code.claude.com/docs/en/memory' cited — evidence class: Unverified (cannot probe URL resolution)
Verbatim quote block is long and specific; transcription fidelity unconfirmable from plan text alone
The empirical canary test is independent corroboration, so this does not create a capability gap, but the citation quality is Unverified
findings.finding: The official-docs citation (`code.claude.com/docs/en/memory`) and its verbatim quoted text are Unverified — the URL cannot be confirmed to resolve, and the quoted passage ('Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md … create CLAUDE.md [that] imports [it]') cannot be validated as accurately transcribed. Downstream stories may treat the doc quote as authoritative when it is only Partially-verified.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'Official docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) confirm and recommend exactly this'
scenarios: The docs URL changes or the quoted behavior is version-gated, leading future readers to rely on a stale citation
The 'max depth = 4 hops' and 'same-repo needs no approval' claims derive solely from this unverified doc and have no empirical backing in the canary test

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
